Bristol Floor Specialists

Floor Sanding in Bristol, Done Properly

A father and son team bringing tired wooden floors back to life across Bristol. Sanding, restoration, insulation and fitting.

Father and son team
15 years of Bristol floors
Finished to last, not rushed
About Us

A Bristol Family Business, 15 Years In

We are Bearwood Design, a father and son team working on wooden floors across Bristol. Between us we have spent 15 years lifting, sanding, repairing and finishing the boards in this city's houses, from Victorian pine in Bishopston to engineered oak in a new Clifton kitchen.

We take on one job at a time and we give it the days it actually needs. No skipped grits, no thin coat of lacquer over a floor that was never prepped properly, no van pulling away before the finish has gone off.

Quality Over Speed, Every Time We would rather do one floor properly than three in a hurry. When we hand a room back it is finished, and it is finished to last.

Why Choose Bearwood Design

Father and son, not subcontractorsThe two people who quote your floor are the two people who sand it.
15 years on Bristol floorsOld pine, parquet, engineered board. We have seen what this city's houses are made of.
Prepped properly before finishingRepairs, gap filling and the full grit sequence come first. The finish is the last step, not the fix.
Clean, dust-extracted sandingWe work in people's homes. We keep the mess where it belongs and clear up after ourselves.
What We Do

Our Services

Wooden floors sanded, restored, insulated and fitted across Bristol. One team, start to finish.

Floor Sanding

Tired, worn boards taken back to bare timber and finished to last.

Floor Insulation

Warmer rooms and less heat lost through the floorboards.

Floor Restoration

Gappy, damaged or patched floors repaired and brought back to life.

Floor Fitting

New solid and engineered floors laid level, tight and square.

Where We Work

Service Areas

We cover Bristol and the surrounding areas. If your street is not on the list, ring us anyway, we are usually not far away.

Clifton Redland Bishopston Cotham Bedminster Henleaze Westbury-on-Trym Keynsham
Common Questions

FAQ

A single room is usually two to three days: sanding, any repairs and gap filling, then the coats of finish with drying time between them. A whole ground floor takes longer. We give you a realistic number on the quote rather than a hopeful one.
Far less than people expect. Our sanders run on dust extraction, we seal off the room where we can, and we clean up at the end of every day. It is still a building job, but you will not be finding dust in the kitchen cupboards a month later.
Usually, yes. Paint, black bitumen, gaps, split boards and old patch repairs are all normal and all fixable. We will tell you honestly if a floor is too far gone rather than take the job and hope.
Yes. Bristol's older houses are full of gappy pine. Depending on the floor we fill with resin and dust or slot in timber slivers, then sand the lot flat so the repair disappears into the board.
Hardwax oil or lacquer, depending on the room and how you live in it. We will talk you through the look and the upkeep of each and let you decide with a sample in front of you.
No. The room being worked on needs to be clear and stay clear while the finish cures, but you can carry on living in the rest of the house. We plan the order of rooms around that.
Because we do the preparation. The grit sequence, the repairs, the gap filling and the drying time between coats are the parts that get cut to make a price look good, and they are the parts that decide whether your floor still looks right in ten years.
Ready When You Are

Let's Get Your Floors Sorted

Give us a ring and tell us about the floor. We will tell you straight what it needs.
